assertions.description: Output has three parts: Headline Summary (2-4 sentences), Key Findings (3-6 bullets with figures), and Methodology and Caveats
Margin of error is stated and applied to at least the headline figure
Any subgroup finding below n=100 is flagged as unreliable

assertion_results.note: Three-part structure with headline (2 sentences), 5 findings with figures, methodology section
Margin of error ~2% stated; 5-point approval drop noted as exceeding MoE
18-29 subgroup (n=340) noted; no subgroups below n=100 in this dataset
MoE ~3.5% for n=800 stated and applied
No subgroups provided so no flagging needed — but assertion about n<100 is not applicable; skill handles this correctly by noting no subgroup data available
MoE calculated per country (~3% for n=1000); applied to headline 3-point trust decline
15-24 age subgroup (n=3,200) is robust and reported as such; no subgroups below n=100

methodology: Binary assertion testing: 3 inputs (standard, minimal, European context) x 3 assertions per skill. Each assertion graded pass/fail based on whether generated output satisfies the requirement.
